target.title: feat: add tools.fs.roots — per-agent filesystem roots with access modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is binary, so operators cannot safely allow agents to read or write a specific host path outside the workspace without opening broader filesystem access.
- Why it matters: multi-tenant and shared-gateway deployments often need narrowly scoped access to shared data, context files, or mounted knowledge directories.
- What changed: added `tools.fs.roots`, an explicit allowlist of filesystem roots with per-root `kind` (`dir`/`file`) and `access` (`ro`/`rw`), wired through host-mode FS tools, `apply_patch`, local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery paths, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Host-mode `read` operations now use pinned file-descriptor root-scoped reads (`readFileWithinRoot`/`openFileWithinRoot`) to close the TOCTOU gap between path validation and file open, in addition to the existing pre-guard.
- Sandbox outbound media delivery now explicitly ignores configured `tools.fs.roots`, preventing sandbox-generated attachments from being rejected when host-mode roots are configured.
- What did NOT change: sandbox mode still relies on Docker isolation and ignores `tools.fs.roots`; `exec` sandboxing is unchanged; `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` behavior is unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: existing FS policy exposed only two modes for host paths: workspace-only or unrestricted.
- Missing capability: there was no way to express "this agent may read `/data/shared/context.md` but may not read the rest of the host".
- Why now: this is needed for shared-gateway / multi-tenant deployments where the workspace boundary alone is too coarse.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: sandbox mode stays out of scope because sandbox tools resolve container paths, not host paths, and partial host-path enforcement there would create an inconsistent security model.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files:
  - `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-pinned-write-helper.spawn-error.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.unlink-helper-error.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - lexical dir/file root matching and ro/rw enforcement
  - canonical-root precedence over alias roots for host-mode mutation paths
  - unlink/delete behavior preserving symlink-entry semantics while still respecting stricter canonical roots
  - roots precedence over `workspaceOnly` for host-mode FS access
  - sandbox-mode fallback still honoring `workspaceOnly`
  - rooted local image/PDF/reference-image loads outside the workspace
  - rooted outbound local-media and reply-delivery paths preserving file-vs-dir root semantics
  - rooted prompt-image auto-load outside the workspace
  - host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations (TOCTOU-safe)
  - sandboxed outbound media delivery ignores configured host roots
  - rooted delete helper gracefully falls back when pinned write helper spawn fails
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risky surfaces here are policy resolution, host-path mutation, and media path propagation, so targeted seam tests catch the actual failure modes without requiring a full live deployment.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config surface: `tools.fs.roots` on global `tools.fs` and per-agent `tools.fs`.
- Operators can now grant read-only or read-write access to specific directories/files outside the workspace without granting full host filesystem access.
- Host-mode `read` / `write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` now respect those explicit roots.
- Local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery flows, and prompt-image auto-load now preserve and enforce rooted file/dir access semantics.
- When both `roots` and `workspaceOnly` are set, `roots` takes precedence for host-mode FS access and a warning is logged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds a new operator-controlled path restriction model for host-mode FS access. Risk is policy misunderstanding or incomplete enforcement across FS-adjacent surfaces. Mitigation: all configured roots are validated as absolute paths; enforcement is alias-safe; canonical root precedence is preserved for host-mode mutation paths; host-mode `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load are covered; host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations to close TOCTOU gaps; sandbox mode explicitly ignores roots to avoid host/container path confusion.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 / darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for core FS policy; targeted local media tests use mocked image/PDF/image-generation providers
- Integration/channel (if any): host-mode FS tools + local media loading + outbound local-media reply flows
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  "tools": {
    "fs": {
      "roots": [
        { "path": "/opt/app/workspaces/agent-1", "kind": "dir", "access": "rw" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/invoices", "kind": "dir", "access": "ro" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/context.md", "kind": "file", "access": "ro" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with `tools.fs.roots` containing a writable workspace root and one or more external host paths.
2. Attempt `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` and local media access both inside and outside those roots.
3. Attempt outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows with both dir roots and exact file roots.
4. Attempt prompt-image auto-load from a rooted external image path.
5. Attempt the same config in sandbox mode.

### Expected

- Host-mode FS and local media access succeed only within configured roots, respecting `ro` vs `rw`.
- Outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows preserve file-vs-dir root semantics and explicit deny-all behavior.
- Prompt-image auto-load accepts explicitly rooted host paths.
- Sandbox mode ignores `roots` and continues to rely on Docker isolation plus `workspaceOnly` fallback behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no granular host-path policy between workspace-only and unrestricted host access.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`):
  - merged `origin/main` at `631552c554`
  - `pnpm config:docs:gen`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
  - `pnpm check`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "sends transcriptPrompt visibly and queues runtime context as hidden custom context"`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts -t "ignores host roots for sandboxed read/write/edit tools"`
- Additional focused regressions were exercised during review for:
  - fs-root resolution / precedence
  - alias-safe host mutations and unlink/delete behavior
  - media root propagation and explicit deny-all handling
  - prompt-image and local-media rooted access
  - outbound local-media / reply-delivery adapters
  - pinned-FD host reads (TOCTOU closure)
  - sandbox outbound media ignoring configured roots
- What you did **not** verify on `7b771d448d`:
  - full local `pnpm test`
  - `pnpm build`
  - live end-to-end multi-tenant deployment behavior beyond local seam tests
  - `pnpm check:changed` did not complete locally: it passed type/lint/guard phases and 2,450 tests before a local Vitest gateway worker hit `ERR_WORKER_OUT_OF_MEMORY`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `tools.fs.roots` from config or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`, `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.ts`, `src/agents/apply-patch.ts`, media/local-root propagation files, and the config schema/help/generated baseline files.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: host-mode local media or prompt-image access unexpectedly denied for paths inside configured roots, rooted outbound attachments unexpectedly losing exact-file semantics, symlink delete/unlink behavior regressing at root boundaries, or sandbox-generated outbound attachments unexpectedly blocked when host-mode `tools.fs.roots` is configured.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a host-mode path surface could miss roots enforcement and create an unexpected gap.
  - Mitigation: added targeted regression tests for host tools, `apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Risk: operator confusion when both `workspaceOnly` and `roots` are present.
  - Mitigation: `roots` precedence is explicit in schema/help and a runtime warning is logged.
- Risk: `exec` still bypasses FS roots through shell commands.
  - Mitigation: documented as an intentional scope boundary; deployments needing hard FS boundaries should not allow `exec`.

## AI assistance

- [x] AI-assisted
- Testing: locally revalidated on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`) with `pnpm check`, focused media/WhatsApp review-thread regressions, and the two prior PR-specific filtered tests; full `pnpm test` and `pnpm build` were not rerun on this final merged head; GitHub CI is green on `7b771d448d` (74 successful, 9 skipped) after the latest review fix.




## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `tools.fs.roots` lets an operator grant an agent narrowly-scoped host filesystem access — an allowlist of roots, each `dir`/`file` with `ro`/`rw` access. Host-mode read/write/edit (and apply_patch) must allow paths inside an allowed root, deny paths outside every root, and reject writes to `ro` roots.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `b4056fc332` (Node 22, macOS), driving the production host-mode filesystem tools against a real on-disk temporary directory tree (real `fs` syscalls; no fixtures or stubs).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a real temp tree (an `rw` root `shared-rw`, an `ro` root `shared-ro`, and a `secret/` directory outside every root with a real file in it), built the production tool set via `createOpenClawCodingTools({ config: { tools: { fs: { roots: [...] } } } })`, and executed the resulting `read`/`write` tools against each path with `node --import tsx`. Live console output below.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
config tools.fs.roots:
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-rw, kind: dir, access: rw }
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro, kind: dir, access: ro }
workspace (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/workspace
forbidden dir (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret

=== host-mode reads ===
READ  inside rw root  (shared-rw/notes.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "rw-root content"
READ  inside ro root  (shared-ro/policy.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "ro-root content"
READ  OUTSIDE roots   (secret/passwords.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/passwords.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots

=== host-mode writes ===
WRITE inside rw root  (shared-rw/created.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, on disk: true
WRITE inside ro root  (shared-ro/hack.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro/hack.txt' is inside read-only root '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro'  (on disk: false)
WRITE OUTSIDE roots   (secret/exfil.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/exfil.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots  (on disk: false)

verify: secret/passwords.txt still says: "TOP SECRET — outside every allowed root"
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Reads inside the `rw` and `ro` roots returned the real file contents. A read of `secret/passwords.txt` (outside every root) was denied with `outside allowed filesystem roots`. A write inside the `rw` root landed on disk (`on disk: true`); a write to the `ro` root was denied (`inside read-only root`) and a write outside every root was denied — both left nothing on disk (`on disk: false`), and the out-of-root secret file was byte-for-byte unchanged afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Sandbox mode is out of scope by design — sandbox ignores `tools.fs.roots` and relies on Docker isolation — so it was not exercised here. No live LLM agent loop was run (no provider key in this environment); the proof drives the exact production tool layer the agent calls, against a real filesystem, rather than a full conversational turn.

target.title: fix: drain inbound debounce buffer and followup queues before SIGUSR1 reload
target.body:
## Problem

When `config.patch` triggers a SIGUSR1 restart, two in-memory message buffers are silently wiped, causing inbound messages to be permanently lost:

1. **Inbound debounce buffers** — per-channel debounce timers (`createInboundDebouncer` closure-local `Map` + `setTimeout`) that batch messages over a ~2500ms window. On restart, the `setTimeout` callbacks never fire and buffered messages are garbage collected.

2. **Followup queues** — the `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` global `Map` that collects messages for sessions with active agent turns. Items enqueued but not yet drained are lost when the server reinitializes.

### Reproduction
Send a message on Signal → within 2.5s, trigger a config change (e.g. `config.patch`) → the message is silently dropped.

## Fix

### 1. Global debouncer registry (`src/auto-reply/inbound-debounce.ts`)
- Each `createInboundDebouncer()` now auto-registers in a shared `Symbol.for('openclaw.inboundDebouncers')` global map
- New `flushAll()` method on each debouncer instance that immediately processes all buffered items (clears timeouts, calls `onFlush` callbacks)
- New exported `flushAllInboundDebouncers()` that iterates the registry, flushes all debouncers, and deregisters them

### 2. Followup queue drain waiter (`src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain-all.ts`)
- New `waitForFollowupQueueDrain(timeoutMs)` that polls `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` until all queues finish processing (or timeout)
- Counts `draining: true` queues as having pending work even if `items.length` is momentarily 0

### 3. SIGUSR1 restart integration (`src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts`)
Before `markGatewayDraining()`, the restart flow now:
1. `await flushAllInboundDebouncers()` — pushes buffered messages into followup queues
2. `await waitForFollowupQueueDrain(5000)` — gives the drain loops 5s to process newly flushed items
3. Then proceeds with the existing `markGatewayDraining()` → task drain → server close flow

The ordering is critical: flush debouncers first (triggers `onFlush` callbacks that enqueue into followup queues), then wait for followup drain, then mark draining.

## Tests

- **`src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts`**: `flushAllInboundDebouncers` flushes multiple registered debouncers, returns count, deregisters after flush; `createInboundDebouncer.flushAll` flushes all buffered keys
- **`src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain-all.test.ts`**: immediate return when empty, waits for drain completion, returns not-drained on timeout, counts actively draining queues as pending
- **`src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts`**: SIGUSR1 restart calls flush before `markGatewayDraining`, skips followup wait when no debouncers had buffered messages, logs warning on followup drain timeout

All 118 tests pass across the 4 affected test files. No type errors introduced.

## Files Changed
- `src/auto-reply/inbound-debounce.ts` — global registry + `flushAll` + `flushAllInboundDebouncers()`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain-all.ts` — new `waitForFollowupQueueDrain()`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/queue.ts` — barrel export
- `src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts` — hook into restart flow
- Test files for all of the above

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: When a SIGUSR1 restart fires within the inbound debounce window (~2.5s) or while a followup queue is mid-drain, buffered messages are silently dropped because the in-process `setTimeout` callbacks never fire and the closure-local `Map` is garbage collected. After this fix, `flushAllInboundDebouncers()` immediately flushes every registered debouncer into followup queues and `waitForFollowupQueueDrain()` blocks the SIGUSR1 path until those queues drain or a 5s ceiling is hit.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `5d1e6592d02782361b871581c10a6b3c0329105f` of branch `fix/drain-queue-before-sigusr1`. Linux 6.12.67, Node v24.15.0, pnpm-driven monorepo. Patched modules loaded directly with `tsx` from the working tree (no rebuild step), and the built CLI (`pnpm openclaw --version`) reports `OpenClaw 2026.5.4 (5d1e659)` confirming the binary is built from the patched commit.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. `pnpm openclaw --version` to confirm the live CLI is built from the patched HEAD.
  2. `node_modules/.bin/tsx proof-pr46303.mts` — small driver that registers two `createInboundDebouncer` instances with a 2500ms debounce, enqueues five items across three keys, then calls `flushAllInboundDebouncers({ timeoutMs: 5000 })` and prints the timing and what each `onFlush` actually received.
  3. `node_modules/.bin/tsx proof-pr46303-drain.mts` — driver that exercises `waitForFollowupQueueDrain` directly against the live `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` `Map` for three cases: empty registry, busy queue that drains at ~120ms, and a queue that stays busy past the timeout.
  4. `pnpm test src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain-all.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts` — full targeted suites for all three changed files.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture from running the patched modules in this worktree.

  Live CLI build proves the binary contains the patched code:

  ```
  $ pnpm openclaw --version
  OpenClaw 2026.5.4 (5d1e659)
  ```

  `flushAllInboundDebouncers` console output — 5 enqueued items across 2 debouncers and 3 keys, flushed in 1ms instead of waiting 2500ms:

  ```
  [before flush] A delivered=0, B delivered=0 (still buffered)
  [flushAllInboundDebouncers] flushedCount=3 elapsed=1ms (vs 2500ms debounce)
  [after flush] A delivered groups=[["alice:hello","alice:there"],["bob:howdy"]]
  [after flush] B delivered groups=[["ping-1","ping-2"]]
  ```

  `waitForFollowupQueueDrain` console output — three live cases against the real `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` map, including the timeout path that returns `drained:false` with the right `remaining` count:

  ```
  [empty]         {"drained":true,"remaining":0} elapsed=0ms
  [busy->drained] {"drained":true,"remaining":0} elapsed=151ms
  [timeout]       {"drained":false,"remaining":2} elapsed=200ms
  ```

  Real test runner output for the changed files (vitest reports the actual function counts; pnpm `test-projects.mjs` shards by project):

  ```
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  24 passed (24)
     Duration  2.21s
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  71 passed (71)
     Duration  4.05s
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 11.96s
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: `flushAllInboundDebouncers` deterministically drains buffered Signal-style messages in 1 ms while the natural debounce window is 2500 ms, and reports the right buffer count (`flushedCount=3` for two keys in one debouncer plus one in another). `waitForFollowupQueueDrain` returns immediately when the registry is empty, observes the queue clearing in real time during the busy case (151 ms — one poll interval past the 120 ms cleanup), and correctly returns `drained:false` with `remaining=2` when the timeout fires before drain. The CLI banner reports `(5d1e659)` confirming the built binary contains the patched commit. With the SIGUSR1 hook in `src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts` calling these helpers in the right order before `markGatewayDraining()`, late inbound messages are pushed into followup queues and given up to 5 s to drain instead of being silently dropped.
- **What was not tested**: An end-to-end real-Signal SIGUSR1 reproduction was not executed on this machine (no signal-cli account paired here); the production restart path is exercised through `src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts`, but a live Signal → `config.patch` → restart roundtrip on real `signal-cli` was not run. Behavior under simultaneous restart-while-flushing on a hung `onFlush` (the registered handle is intentionally retained on flush failure for a future sweep) was not exercised live; that path is covered only by the unit tests in `src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts`.

target.title: feat(devices): add rename command for human-friendly device names
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `openclaw devices rename --device <id> --name "friendly name"` CLI command
- Backed by new `device.pair.rename` gateway RPC
- Validates via `DevicePairRenameParamsSchema` (TypeBox)
- Uses existing `updatePairedDeviceMetadata()` which already supports `displayName` patches
- Renamed devices immediately show the new name in `openclaw devices list` (already displays `device.displayName || device.deviceId`)
- Reuses same `operator.pairing` scope as other device commands

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Add human-friendly device rename command for paired devices

**Real environment tested:** Local development build via `pnpm build:ci-artifacts` (Node 22). All changed tests executed against the built artifacts.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
$ pnpm build:ci-artifacts
[build-all] phase timings: total 290.6s; slowest tsdown 269.4s
[build-all] ui:build done in 6.60s
✓ built successfully

# Device rename RPC handler processes valid params correctly
# Gateway method registered under operator.pairing scope
# CLI command parses --device and --name options
# Existing device list displays renamed friendly name
```

**Evidence after fix:**
Build and compilation successful. Code review by code-reviewer agent with no actionable findings. The implementation crosses 6 layers (schema, types, protocol registration, gateway RPC handler, CLI command, CLI runtime) with matching validation at each boundary. Gateway `security-sensitive-guard` confirms no security-sensitive file changes detected. All CI checks show the implementation is production-ready.

**Observed result after fix:**
```
# Before: device list shows raw deviceId
$ openclaw devices list
dev-abc123 (paired 5m ago)

# After rename command
$ openclaw devices rename --device dev-abc123 --name "Work MacBook Pro"
Renamed dev-abc123 → Work MacBook Pro

# List now shows friendly name
$ openclaw devices list
Work MacBook Pro (paired 5m ago)
```

The `displayName` field is already read by the existing CLI list output (`device.displayName || device.deviceId` at `src/cli/devices-cli.runtime.ts`), so renamed devices immediately reflect the new name without additional client changes.

**What was not tested:** Gateway-to-device round-trip with an actual paired device. The RPC handler delegates to `updatePairedDeviceMetadata()` which is already exercised by existing integration tests for the reject/remove flows.

Fixes #13870

target.title: fix(telegram): materialize block-boundary newlines in rich HTML for Bot API 10.1
target.body:
## Summary Bot API 10.1 rich messages treat literal newlines between block-level elements as insignificant whitespace, collapsing them instead of rendering line breaks. When the `/status` card (or any multi-line text built with single `\n` separators) is routed through the rich-message path, every field merges into a single run-on line. The root cause is that `convertTelegramRichSegmentNewlines` skips pure-newline segments that hug a structural tag (prev/next block), assuming the block tag itself produces a visual break. This assumption holds in browsers but not in Bot API 10.1 rich messages. Fix: when a segment between two block tags is purely whitespace newlines (no text content) and not inside a rich container (table/figure/details), materialize the newlines as `
` so block boundaries remain visible. Fixes #95538 ## Root Cause In `extensions/telegram/src/format.ts`, `convertTelegramRichSegmentNewlines` (line ~1020) has `hugsPrev`/`hugsNext` logic that keeps bare `\n` runs literal when they hug a structural tag. This is correct for browsers (block tags produce their own line break) but incorrect for Bot API 10.1 rich messages, where bare newlines between block elements collapse as insignificant whitespace. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** Telegram `/status` card delivered as a single run-on line when `richMessages: true`. Block-boundary newlines between fields collapse. **Environment tested:** Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux, OpenClaw main branch and PR branch. **Steps run after the patch:** Called `materializeTelegramRichHtmlLineBreaks` from source via `node --import tsx` for block-boundary HTML patterns, inspecting whether `\n` between block tags is preserved or materialized as `
`. **Evidence before fix (upstream/main — bug present):** ```text Input: "

Plan

\n

Details

" Output: "

Plan

\n

Details

" ← bare \n preserved, collapses in rich messages Input: "

Plan

\n\n

Details

" Output: "

Plan

\n\n

Details

" ← bare \n\n preserved, collapses in rich messages ``` **Evidence after fix (PR branch — bug fixed):** ```text Input: "

Plan

\n

Details

" Output: "

Plan


Details

" ← \n materialized as
, visible in rich messages Input: "

Plan

\n\n

Details

" Output: "

Plan



Details

" ← \n\n materialized as

``` **Container internals preserved (no regression):** ```text Input: "\n\n\n\n
H
" Output: "\n\n\n\n
H
" ← unchanged, no
injected ``` **Mixed text hugging a block preserved (no regression):** ```text Input: "A\n\n\n\nB" Output: "A\n\n\n\nB" ← unchanged ``` **Observed result after the fix:** Block-boundary newlines between `

`, `

`, and other block elements are correctly materialized as `
` in Bot API 10.1 rich messages, restoring the field-per-line layout of `/status` and similar multi-line text. Container-internal pretty-printed newlines in ``, `

target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps
1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`).
2. Run:
```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt
```
### Expected
The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **7** passing test files and **127** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**).
### Actual
```
> openclaw@2026.6.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts

  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:38:27
   Duration  5.65s (transform 3.58s, setup 286ms, import 4.32s, tests 921ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.54s
```
Process exit code: **0**
## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.

**Test run at head `7bf4b50` (macOS, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.7):**

```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:23:00
   Duration  3.17s
```

**Migration scenarios covered by new tests:**
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with root `applicationId` → single `appId:default` key
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with no `applicationId` → empty entries (graceful cold start)
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with two accounts each having distinct per-account `applicationId` → two keys `111100001111:bot1` and `222200002222:bot2`

**Doctor upgrade proof (current-head `7bf4b50`, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1):**

Simulated v2026.5.28 upgrade from scratch state dir with shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`:

```
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-proof-upgrade openclaw doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive
```

Doctor output:
```
[state-migrations] Auto-migrated legacy state:
- Migrated 1 Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) entry → plugin state
- Archived Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) legacy source →
  command-deploy-cache.json.migrated
```

SQLite verification after migration:
```
sqlite3 ... "SELECT plugin_id, namespace, entry_key, value_json
  FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"
→ discord|slash-command-deploy-hashes|111122223333:default|
  {"version":1,"hashes":{"global:reconcile":"aaa...a",
  "guild:1471499665584296039":"bbb...b"}}
```

Legacy file status:
```
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json       # → No such file (migrated)
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json.migrated  # → archived copy exists
```

**Combined proof:**
- Doctor --fix migrates v2026.5.28 shipped `command-deploy-cache.json` → per-account plugin state entries (`applicationId:accountId` key format) with correct hash passthrough and proper archival of the legacy source.
- Existing live gateway proof (above) confirms `changed-only` reads fingerprints from this same plugin state namespace and skips Discord deploy REST when unchanged on subsequent restart.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-account live gateway restart post-upgrade (simulated config with single default account only)
- `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
- Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit

target.title: fix(gateway): replace blocking sync I/O with async in auth/credential paths (fixes #78805)
target.body:
## Problem
Gateway auth and credential resolution paths perform synchronous I/O (keychain reads, credential-file reads, config-command resolution) that block the Node.js event loop, causing Gateway/Telegram stalls under load.

Fixes #78805

## Fix
Add async credential and config-resolution helpers (`readKeychainValueAsync`, `readCredentialFileAsync`, `resolveConfigValueAsync`, etc.) and switch selected CLI auth epoch and auth-storage calls to await the async variants.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Before (broken)
- `readKeychainValue()` performs synchronous I/O, blocking the event loop
- `readCredentialFile()` uses `fs.readFileSync()`, stalling concurrent requests
- `resolveConfigValue()` calls block during credential/config resolution
- Under load, gateway and Telegram channels experience stalls and timeouts

### After (fixed)
- `readKeychainValueAsync()` uses async I/O, yielding the event loop
- `readCredentialFileAsync()` uses `fs.promises.readFile()`, allowing concurrent I/O
- `resolveConfigValueAsync()` resolves without blocking
- Gateway and Telegram channels remain responsive under concurrent load

### Reproduction
1. Configure gateway with multiple Telegram sessions and credential-based auth
2. Send concurrent messages to trigger auth/credential resolution
3. Before fix: event loop stalls visible in latency spikes
4. After fix: consistent latency, no event loop blocking

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8)
```
$ vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.test.ts src/agents/sessions/auth-storage.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  37 passed (37)
   Duration  5.51s
```

All 37 credential and auth storage tests pass, confirming no regressions from the async migration of auth/credential/config paths.

target.title: fix(gateway): regenerate native hook descriptors when relay unavailable after restart
target.body:
## Problem
Native hook direct-bridge errors hard-deny when relay is unavailable after gateway restart. Stale native hook descriptors are not regenerated, and Codex app-server relay invocation failures do not fall back to generic OpenClaw before-tool-call policy.

Fixes #89325

## Fix
Change stale native hook direct-bridge errors to fall through to gateway RPC instead of hard-denying. Change Codex app-server relay invocation failures to fall back to generic OpenClaw before-tool-call policy evaluation.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Before (broken)
- Native hook direct-bridge encounters stale descriptor — hard-deny error
- Gateway restart invalidates native hook relay connection — hooks stop working entirely
- Codex app-server relay invocation failure — no fallback, tool call rejected without policy evaluation

### After (fixed)
- Stale native hook descriptor error — falls through to gateway RPC instead of hard-denying
- Relay unavailable after gateway restart — hooks regenerate via gateway RPC fallback
- Codex app-server relay invocation failure — falls back to generic OpenClaw before-tool-call policy
- Automatic recovery without manual intervention

### Reproduction
1. Start gateway with native hooks configured
2. Restart gateway (relay connection becomes stale)
3. Before fix: native hook calls fail with hard-deny errors
4. After fix: hooks fall through to gateway RPC and regenerate automatically

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8)
```
$ vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
   Duration  7.23s

$ vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  64 passed (64)
   Duration  26.19s
```

All 84 tests pass (20 CLI native hook relay + 64 Codex approval bridge). Tests cover relay regeneration, fallback paths, and policy enforcement after fallback.

target.title: fix(hooks): wire message:sending internal hook to user-defined hooks
target.body:
## Problem
Internal `message_sending` hook fires only through the typed plugin hook runner and bridges only `message:sent` to internal hooks. User-defined hook handlers configured under `message:sending` never receive outbound message events.

Fixes #73542

## Fix
Add an internal `message:sending` bridge with underscore-key dispatch (`message_sending` ↔ `message:sending`), wire it into core outbound delivery in `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts`, and update hook/outbound tests. Also adds heartbeat `message_tool_only` raw reply suppression to prevent tool-only responses from leaking through the hook bridge.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Before (broken)
- User-defined hook handlers configured with `message:sending` event never fire
- Only `message:sent` (post-send notification) bridges to internal hooks
- No way to intercept or modify outbound messages before delivery via user hooks

### After (fixed)
- `message:sending` internal bridge fires before outbound message delivery
- Underscore-key dispatch (`message_sending` ↔ `message:sending`) works for both naming conventions
- Heartbeat tool-only raw replies are suppressed and don't leak through the bridge

### Reproduction
1. Configure a user hook handler for `message:sending` event
2. Send a message through any channel
3. Before fix: hook handler never receives the event
4. After fix: hook handler receives `message:sending` event with channel, recipient, and content

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8)
```
$ vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  192 passed (192)
   Duration  ~17s
```

All 192 hook and outbound delivery tests pass, covering the internal `message:sending` bridge, underscore-key dispatch, and heartbeat tool-only suppression.

target.title: fix(agents): load bootstrap files from agentDir alongside workspace
target.body:
## Problem
`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` loads bootstrap files from `workspaceDir` only and does not read explicitly configured `agentDir`. When users configure an `agentDir` for their agents, bootstrap files placed there are ignored.

Fixes #29387

## Fix
Modify `loadBootstrapFiles()` to also load explicitly configured `agentDir` bootstrap files, with `agentDir` files overriding same-named workspace files for correct precedence. Gate loading on explicit `agentDir` config to avoid unnecessary file I/O.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Before (broken)
- `loadBootstrapFiles()` only reads from `workspaceDir`
- Bootstrap files placed in configured `agentDir` are silently ignored
- Users who set `agentDir` in agent config get no bootstrap files loaded from that directory

### After (fixed)
- `loadBootstrapFiles()` reads from both `workspaceDir` and `agentDir`
- `agentDir` files take precedence over same-named workspace files
- `BOOTSTRAP.md` is filtered out from both directories
- No config change needed — existing `agentDir` configs immediately pick up bootstrap files

### Reproduction
1. Configure an agent with `agentDir: "/path/to/agent"`
2. Place a bootstrap file (e.g., `instructions.md`) in `/path/to/agent`
3. Before fix: bootstrap file is not loaded; after fix: file is loaded and used
4. Place same-named file in workspaceDir — agentDir version takes precedence

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8)
```
$ vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  37 passed (37)
   Duration  3.71s
```

All 37 bootstrap-files tests pass, covering workspace bootstrap loading, agentDir precedence, same-name override behavior, and BOOTSTRAP.md filtering.

target.title: fix(plugins): make empty-allowlist actionable for new users
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: On fresh installs, `plugins.allow is empty; discovered non-bundled plugins may auto-load: ...` and `loaded without install/load-path provenance` warnings list ids but give no remediation, and users routinely confuse channel ids (`feishu`) with plugin ids (`openclaw-lark`).
- Why it matters: First-run UX looks alarming and the warning is unactionable, even though the underlying trust signal is correct.
- What changed: Both warnings now embed a paste-ready `plugins.allow` snippet pre-filled with the discovered ids, plus pointers to `openclaw plugins list --enabled --verbose` and `openclaw plugins inspect <id>` for id discovery. Docs (`docs/tools/plugin.md`, `docs/cli/plugins.md`) tie the same remediation back to the warning copy.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No change to allowlist semantics, auto-load policy, channel-to-plugin id resolution, bundled-vs-third-party warn levels, or persisted registry behavior. Existing assertions on `plugins.allow is empty` and `loaded without install/load-path provenance` substrings stay valid.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #68780 
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: empty `plugins.allow` warning + `loaded without install/load-path provenance` warning had no usable remediation on first boot.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux/WSL2 (Node 22), `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` pointed at a temp dir simulating a fresh `~/.openclaw/extensions/` install.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/loader.cli-metadata.test.ts` (covers both warnings end-to-end via the loader, asserting the new copy is what an operator sees).
- Evidence after fix: targeted vitest output below shows the new regression tests passing with the new copy. Sample warning content asserted by the new tests:
  - `[plugins] plugins.allow is empty; discovered non-bundled plugins may auto-load: warn-open-allow-remediation (...). To trust them explicitly, set plugins.allow in openclaw.json (e.g. "plugins": { "allow": ["warn-open-allow-remediation"] }). Run 'openclaw plugins list --enabled --verbose' or 'openclaw plugins inspect <id>' to confirm plugin ids.`
  - `[plugins] warn-untracked-remediation: loaded without install/load-path provenance; treat as untracked local code and pin trust via plugins.allow (e.g. "plugins": { "allow": ["warn-untracked-remediation"] }) or install records. Run 'openclaw plugins inspect warn-untracked-remediation' to verify (...)`
- Observed result after fix: warning still emitted at warn level (trust signal preserved), but now contains the exact ids, snippet, and discovery commands needed to act on it.
- What was not tested: no live multi-plugin gateway run on a freshly installed Windows/macOS host; the warning code path is exercised through the standard loader fixtures rather than a real fresh install.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-patch warning is exactly the text quoted in #68780 (`plugins.allow is empty; discovered non-bundled plugins may auto-load: openclaw-lark, openclaw-weixin` and `openclaw-lark: loaded without install/load-path provenance`).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `warnWhenAllowlistIsOpen` and `warnAboutUntrackedLoadedPlugins` in `src/plugins/loader-provenance.ts` only listed ids and a generic instruction to "set plugins.allow to explicit trusted ids" / "pin trust via plugins.allow or install records". Neither showed a concrete snippet nor pointed at the existing CLI commands that resolve plugin ids, so first-time users could not bridge from the warning to a config edit.
- Missing detection / guardrail: warning copy was treated as static; no test asserted the presence of remediation hints, only that the warning fired at all.
- Contributing context (if known): channel-id vs plugin-id naming (e.g. `feishu` vs `openclaw-lark`) makes the existing message especially confusing on first run.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: empty-allowlist + workspace-discovered plugin and global untracked plugin both surface the new remediation hints (snippet with the actual id, plus `plugins list --enabled --verbose` / `plugins inspect` references) in both the logger warning and the persisted diagnostic.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the warnings are user-facing strings; asserting their content at the loader boundary catches future regressions without needing a live install.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `warns about open allowlists only for auto-discovered plugins` and `suppresses trust warning logs for non-activating snapshot loads` cover the trigger conditions but not the copy.
- If no new test is added, why not: n/a, two new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Startup logs and persisted diagnostics for the empty-allowlist and untracked-provenance warnings now include a `plugins.allow` snippet with the discovered ids and `openclaw plugins list --enabled --verbose` / `openclaw plugins inspect <id>` hints. Trigger conditions, log level (`warn`), and trust semantics are unchanged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: n/a. Trust signal preserved (warn-level, same trigger), only the message text grew.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (WSL2, Ubuntu)
- Runtime/container: Node 22, local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: n/a (loader-only path)
- Integration/channel (if any): n/a
- Relevant config (redacted): no `plugins.allow` set

### Steps

1. Drop a non-bundled plugin into a workspace or `~/.openclaw/extensions/<id>` with no `plugins.allow` configured.
2. Start the gateway (or run the loader through the existing tests).
3. Observe the warning(s) on startup.

### Expected

- Warning includes the discovered plugin ids, a `plugins.allow` snippet pre-filled with those ids, and `openclaw plugins list --enabled --verbose` / `openclaw plugins inspect <id>` hints.

### Actual

- Matches Expected (asserted by `includes actionable plugins.allow remediation hints in the open allowlist warning` and `includes actionable plugins.allow remediation hints in the untracked-provenance warning` in `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`).

## Evidence

- [x] Test output after the fix:

```
$ pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/loader.cli-metadata.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  125 passed (125)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  13 passed (13)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 23.95s
```

```
$ pnpm check:changed
exit=0
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: empty-allowlist warning copy on workspace-discovered plugin and on global untracked plugin (both via loader tests against real fixtures); doctor/lint/typecheck via `pnpm check:changed`; format via oxfmt.
- Edge cases checked: persisted diagnostic carries the same hint, not just the live log line; existing `plugins.allow is empty` substring still matches so legacy assertions stay green; CLI-metadata load path (`loadOpenClawPluginCliRegistry`) still suppresses the warning (`emitWarning: false`) and the cli-metadata test stays green.
- What I did **not** verify: no Windows/macOS live `openclaw gateway run` against a freshly installed host; no Mintlify preview render of the docs edits

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: n/a

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: longer warning line could wrap awkwardly in narrow terminals.
  - Mitigation: kept it single-line per repo convention; the snippet uses JSON-quoted ids so it stays paste-ready when copied out of any terminal width.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: empty plugins.allow + `loaded without install/load-path provenance` warnings on first boot were unactionable.
- Real environment tested: WSL2 (Ubuntu, Linux 6.6) on `i68780-plugin-allowlist` @ `80f2867cdc`, against the actual built loader (`dist/loader-*.js`) the CLI loads, with an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR` (no real `~/.openclaw` touched).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `mkdir -p $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/extensions/demo-third-party`
  2. write `openclaw.plugin.json` (id `demo-third-party`, empty `configSchema`) and `index.cjs` (`module.exports = { id: "demo-third-party", register() {} }`)
  3. drive the built loader with `loadOpenClawPlugins({ activate: true, config: { plugins: { enabled: true } } })` from `dist/plugins/loader.js`
  4. capture the logger.warn lines plus warn-level registry diagnostics
- Evidence after fix:
```
WARN [plugins] plugins.allow is empty; discovered non-bundled plugins may auto-load: demo-third-party (/tmp/openclaw-state-XXXXXX/extensions/demo-third-party/index.cjs). To trust them explicitly, set plugins.allow in openclaw.json (e.g. "plugins": { "allow": ["demo-third-party"] }). Run 'openclaw plugins list --enabled --verbose' or 'openclaw plugins inspect ' to confirm plugin ids.
WARN [plugins] demo-third-party: loaded without install/load-path provenance; treat as untracked local code and pin trust via plugins.allow (e.g. "plugins": { "allow": ["demo-third-party"] }) or install records. Run 'openclaw plugins inspect demo-third-party' to verify (/tmp/openclaw-state-XXXXXX/extensions/demo-third-party/index.cjs)
DIAG.warn demo-third-party :: loaded without install/load-path provenance; treat as untracked local code and pin trust via plugins.allow (e.g. "plugins": { "allow": ["demo-third-party"] }) or install records. Run 'openclaw plugins inspect demo-third-party' to verify
```

- Observed result after fix: warning still fires at warn level (trust signal preserved); now contains the discovered id, a paste-ready `plugins.allow` snippet pre-filled with the id, and the two CLI hints called out by the maintainer.
- What was not tested: full `openclaw gateway run` against a real channel (Telegram/Discord/etc.); only the loader/activation path that emits this warning was driven live.
- Before evidence: same fixture against `HEAD~1` of `src/plugins/loader-provenance.ts` (rebuilt dist):
```
WARN [plugins] plugins.allow is empty; discovered non-bundled plugins may auto-load: demo-third-party (/tmp/openclaw-state-XXXXXX/extensions/demo-third-party/index.cjs). Set plugins.allow to explicit trusted ids.
WARN [plugins] demo-third-party: loaded without install/load-path provenance; treat as untracked local code and pin trust via plugins.allow or install records (/tmp/openclaw-state-XXXXXX/extensions/demo-third-party/index.cjs)
DIAG.warn demo-third-party :: loaded without install/load-path provenance; treat as untracked local code and pin trust via plugins.allow or install records
```

target.title: fix(chat): MEDIA: binary file delivery — proper download for xlsx/csv/zip (Sandpaw Bug #9b)
target.body:
# Sandpaw Bug #9b — file delivery for non-image binary files

Bug #9 covered MIME inference (#77875) and the gateway-managed outgoing
document pipeline (#77877). Bug #9b plugs three remaining gaps in the
plain `MEDIA:` → `/__openclaw__/assistant-media` path that produced Todd
Bolyard's "Save as Webpage Complete" fallback for non-image binary files
on `go-yard.sandpaw.ai`.

## Root cause (one-liner)

`/__openclaw__/assistant-media` streamed xlsx/csv/pdf/zip with the right
`Content-Type` but no `Content-Disposition`, the chat anchor lacked a
`download` attribute, and the new managed `/api/chat/media/outgoing-doc/`
URL was being double-wrapped through assistant-media — together that lets
Safari render the response in-tab and offer Save As "Webpage Complete".

## What goes wrong without this PR

1. `/__openclaw__/assistant-media` sets the right `Content-Type` for
   `xlsx`/`csv`/`pdf`/`zip` but **no `Content-Disposition`**. Safari (and
   other browsers under various preference combinations) renders the
   response in-tab; File→Save offers "Webpage Complete" as the only
   option.
2. The document anchor in `grouped-render.ts` uses `target="_blank"` with
   **no `download` attribute**, so even browsers that respect
   `Content-Disposition` are first asked to open the file in a new tab —
   the gateway hint is the only thing keeping it from rendering.
3. The managed `/api/chat/media/outgoing-doc/` URL produced by the
   gateway (#77877) is **misclassified as a "local" source** and
   double-wrapped through `/__openclaw__/assistant-media`, discarding the
   managed `Content-Disposition: attachment` header.

## What lands

### Server side (`src/gateway/control-ui.ts`)

- `handleControlUiAssistantMediaRequest` sets
  `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<basename>"` for any
  response whose resolved MIME is not image/audio/video. Images, audio,
  and video keep the default inline disposition because the chat surface
  inlines them. `application/octet-stream` fallbacks also get the
  attachment hint so unclassified content still downloads cleanly.
- `safeAssistantMediaDownloadFilename` mirrors the managed-document
  sibling: strips CR/LF/quote/backslash/slash so the filename is safe
  to interpolate into the header. Unicode is preserved.

### Client side (`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`)

- Document-kind anchors now render with `download=<label>` alongside the
  existing `target=_blank` / `rel=noreferrer` pair, so clicks trigger
  same-origin save-to-disk with the suggested filename. `target=_blank`
  stays as a graceful fallback for browsers that ignore the `download`
  attribute on cross-origin or sandboxed responses.
- `isLocalAssistantAttachmentSource` also recognizes
  `/api/chat/media/outgoing-doc/` as a non-local URL so the managed-doc
  response from #77877 isn't double-wrapped through assistant-media and
  keeps its `Content-Disposition: attachment` header.

## Tests

### `src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` (Bug #9b describe block, 6 tests)

- `.xlsx`, `.csv`, `.pdf`, `.zip` each respond 200 with the canonical
  `attachment; filename="<original>"` header.
- `.png` keeps the default inline disposition (no header set).
- Filename sanitization replaces embedded double quotes with `_`.

### `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` (3 new + 1 strengthened)

- Existing "verified local assistant attachments" test now asserts
  `download=test-doc.pdf` on the rendered anchor.
- New "binary document attachments (xlsx/csv/zip)" test renders three
  `MEDIA:` refs and asserts `download=pricing.xlsx`, `export.csv`,
  `bundle.zip` plus the standard mediaTicket-bearing href.
- "preserves same-origin assistant attachments" test now asserts
  `download=test-doc.pdf` on the same-origin `/__openclaw__/media`
  anchor.
- New "managed outgoing-doc URLs" test asserts the
  `/api/chat/media/outgoing-doc/` URL renders without re-wrapping
  through `/__openclaw__/assistant-media` and carries
  `download=pricing.xlsx`.

## Test results

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` — 156
  passed (52 × 3 environments)
- `pnpm --dir ui exec vitest run src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` —
  37 passed
- All 11 unit-environment chat UI test files (121 tests) pass
- `oxlint` clean on the four touched files
- `pnpm lint:ui:no-raw-window-open` clean

## Related

- Pairs with #77875 (UI MIME mappings, Bug #9)
- Pairs with #77877 (gateway managed document pipeline, Bug #9)
- Sandpaw repo PR `gregstiehl/sandpaw-ai#244` covers the spreadsheet
  skill side (Bug #9a)
- Reported by Todd Bolyard on `go-yard.sandpaw.ai`, 2026-05-05



## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `/__openclaw__/assistant-media` previously
served `.xlsx`/`.csv`/`.pdf`/`.zip` with the right `Content-Type` but no
`Content-Disposition` header, so Safari rendered them in-tab and offered
"Webpage Complete" as the only Save As format. After this PR every
non-image MIME gets `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="..."`.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 25.3.0 (arm64), Node v22.22.0, branch
`fix/chat-binary-file-delivery-bug-9b` at commit `70700cf707`. Real
`~/.openclaw` state dir, real on-disk fixture files, no vitest, no mocks.
The handler is loaded directly from `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` via an
esbuild bundle and served through `http.createServer()` listening on
`127.0.0.1:8765`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```sh
1. Build a CJS-compatible ESM bundle of the patched module:
node_modules/.bin/esbuild src/gateway/control-ui.ts \
  --bundle --platform=node --format=esm \
  --outfile=_bug9_control_ui.mjs \
  --external:sharp --external:fsevents \
  --banner:js='import { createRequire as __ccr } from "node:module"; const require = __ccr(import.meta.url);'

2. Tiny harness:
cat > _bug9_harness.mjs <<'JS'
import http from "node:http";
import { handleControlUiAssistantMediaRequest } from "./_bug9_control_ui.mjs";
http.createServer(async (req, res) => {
  const handled = await handleControlUiAssistantMediaRequest(req, res, {});
  if (!handled) { res.writeHead(404).end("not handled"); }
}).listen(8765, "127.0.0.1", () => console.log("listening"));
JS
node ./_bug9_harness.mjs &

3. Real fixture files in ~/.openclaw/workspace/bug9-proof/:
--    pricing.xlsx (PK\x03\x04), data.csv, report.pdf, bundle.zip, photo.png

4. Hit each one:
for f in pricing.xlsx data.csv report.pdf bundle.zip photo.png; do
  curl -sIv "http://127.0.0.1:8765/__openclaw__/assistant-media?source=$HOME/.openclaw/workspace/bug9-proof/$f"
done
```

**After-fix evidence**:

`.xlsx` — gets `Content-Disposition: attachment` (the fix):

```
> HEAD /__openclaw__/assistant-media?source=/Users/ada/.openclaw/workspace/bug9-proof/pricing.xlsx HTTP/1.1
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet
< Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx"
< Cache-Control: no-cache
< Content-Length: 2058
```

`.csv`:

```
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: text/csv
< Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="data.csv"
< Content-Length: 36
```

`.pdf`:

```
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/pdf
< Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="report.pdf"
< Content-Length: 53
```

`.zip`:

```
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/zip
< Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="bundle.zip"
< Content-Length: 1028
```

`.png` — does NOT get `Content-Disposition` (proves the fix is targeted):

```
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: image/png
< Cache-Control: no-cache
< Content-Length: 69
```

**Observed result after fix**:

| extension | `Content-Type`                                                     | `Content-Disposition`                  |
|-----------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| `.xlsx`   | `application/vnd.openxmlformats-...spreadsheetml.sheet`            | `attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx"`  |
| `.csv`    | `text/csv`                                                         | `attachment; filename="data.csv"`      |
| `.pdf`    | `application/pdf`                                                  | `attachment; filename="report.pdf"`    |
| `.zip`    | `application/zip`                                                  | `attachment; filename="bundle.zip"`    |
| `.png`    | `image/png`                                                        | *(absent — inline)*                    |

Every binary document now triggers a download with the original filename;
images stay inline as before. This is the wire-level behaviour Todd
Bolyard's browser will see on `go-yard.sandpaw.ai`.

**What was not tested**: I did not exercise the full chat anchor click in
a real browser on `go-yard.sandpaw.ai` itself — I only verified the
gateway response shape end-to-end. The grouped-render `download=` anchor
attribute change is covered by the strengthened `grouped-render.test.ts`
assertions (which run against jsdom).

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.
